source_date_epoch_effectiveness.workdir_layer: STABILIZED — directory creation timestamp clamped to epoch
source_date_epoch_effectiveness.copy_layers: NOT STABILIZED — BuildKit uses host file mtimes for COPY tar entries; SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH does not clamp them
source_date_epoch_effectiveness.run_layers: NOT STABILIZED — execution outputs (pip .dist-info, /etc/passwd) have real-time creation timestamps from within the container

what_source_date_epoch_fixes: Directories created by WORKDIR (clamped to epoch)
what_source_date_epoch_does_not_fix: COPY layer tar entries: BuildKit embeds source file mtimes in the tar, not the epoch value
RUN pip install: pip writes .dist-info/RECORD and __pycache__ with real build timestamps unless SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH is passed INTO the container
RUN addgroup/adduser: /etc/shadow and /etc/passwd modification times are set by the container runtime at execution time
root_cause_analysis.fix_for_copy_layers: Normalize source file mtimes to epoch before the docker build context is assembled. On Linux: git ls-files -z | xargs -0 touch -d @$SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH. Must run before docker build.
root_cause_analysis.fix_for_run_layers: Pass SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH into the container using Dockerfile ARG+ENV so pip and other tools inside the build respect it.

next_required_changes: Normalize source file mtimes before docker build — fixes COPY layers 6 and 8
Pass SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH into container via Dockerfile ARG+ENV — fixes RUN layers 7 and 9
